claimant_lives_in_uk.description: Whether the claimant lives in the UK.

children.description: A list of children the claimant is claiming for.

lives_with_claimant.description: Whether the child lives with the claimant.

in_approved_education.description: Whether the child is in approved education or training (e.g. A-levels, GCSEs, NVQ up to level 3). Only relevant for children aged 16-19.

in_extension_period.description: Whether the child (aged 16 or 17) has left education and registered with a government-sponsored careers service or the armed forces (qualifying for a 20-week extension).

upkeep_per_week.description: The amount in £ per week the claimant contributes towards the child's upkeep (food, clothes, pocket money, etc.). Only relevant if the child does not live with the claimant.

another_claimant_has_priority.description: Whether another person who also lives with the child has priority for claiming Child Benefit.

another_claimant_lives_with_child.description: Whether someone who lives with the child is already claiming Child Benefit for them.

care_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in local authority care.

care_home_24h_per_week.description: Whether the child spends at least 24 hours per week at home while in local authority care.

hospital_weeks.description: The number of weeks the child has been in hospital or residential accommodation.

claimant_spends_on_child.description: Whether the claimant regularly spends money on the child (e.g. food, clothes, books) while the child is in hospital or residential accommodation.

is_fostered.description: Whether the child is fostered by the claimant.

council_pays_for_child.description: Whether the local council pays towards the child's accommodation or maintenance.

works_24_plus_hours.description: Whether the child works 24 or more hours per week in paid employment.

started_apprenticeship_in_england.description: Whether the child has started an apprenticeship in England.

receives_qualifying_benefits.description: Whether the child receives qualifying benefits in their own right, such as Universal Credit or Employment and Support Allowance.

test_case.agent_script:
=== YOUR SITUATION PROFILE ===
You live in the UK.
You are inquiring about your 1 child:
  - Alex is 6 years old and lives with you.

Here are the exact details of your circumstances to use when answering the agent's questions:

Regarding Alex:
  - Claimant lives in the UK
  - Child is 6 (under 16)
  - Child lives with claimant
  - Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
  - Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
  - Child is not fostered
  - Child does not work 24+ hours/week
  - Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
  - Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

expected_eligibility.name: Alex
expected_eligibility.reason: Claimant lives in the UK; Child is 6 (under 16); Child lives with claimant; Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit); Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation; Child is not fostered; Child does not work 24+ hours/week; Child has not started an apprenticeship in England; Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right
circumstances: Child in care for 7 weeks (within 8-week limit)
Child is not in hospital or residential accommodation
Child has not started an apprenticeship in England
Child does not receive qualifying benefits in their own right

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Hello! I'd be happy to help you determine whether you're eligible for Child Benefit. To do this properly, I'll need to gather some information about you and your children.

Let me start with the first question:

**How many children do you have that you'd like me to assess for Child Benefit eligibility?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Hi! I'd like to find out if I'm eligible for Child Benefit. I have one child - my son Alex.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information! I have one child to assess - your son Alex.

To help determine eligibility, I need to gather some details about Alex's circumstances.

**What is Alex's date of birth?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Alex is 6 years old - he was born in 2019.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you! So Alex was born in 2019 and is currently 6 years old.

**Does Alex live with you full-time?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, he lives at home with me.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Good, thank you for confirming that.

Now I need to understand your residency and immigration status, as this affects Child Benefit eligibility.

**Are you a British citizen or a citizen of another European Economic Area (EEA) country, and do you have the right to reside in the UK?**

conversation.author: actor
conversation.text: Yes, I live in the UK and have the right to reside here.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Thank you for that information. That's helpful.

Before I make my final assessment, let me check the specific eligibility criteria by reviewing the official guidance.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Based on the information you've provided and the official eligibility criteria, I can now make my assessment.

From what you've told me:
- Alex is 6 years old (born in 2019)
- Alex lives with you full-time
- You live in the UK and have the right to reside here
- You are responsible for Alex

According to the Child Benefit eligibility rules, you qualify if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. You're normally responsible for a child if you live with them. Alex is under 16, lives with you, and you live in the UK with the right to reside - so all the key criteria are met.

conversation.author: eligibility_agent
conversation.text:
Excellent news! **You are eligible for Child Benefit for Alex.**

Here's why:
- Alex is 6 years old, which means he's under 16
- He lives with you full-time
- You have the right to reside in the UK
- You are responsible for him

These are the key requirements for Child Benefit eligibility, and you meet all of them. You should be able to claim Child Benefit for Alex. If you'd like to proceed with a claim, you can do so through HMRC's official channels.

reasonings: Alex is 6 years old and therefore under 16. He lives with you full-time, making you responsible for him. You live in the UK and have the right to reside there. According to the eligibility criteria, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. All these conditions are satisfied, so Alex is eligible for Child Benefit.
arguments.overall_reasoning: You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the key requirements: you're responsible for a child under 16, the child lives with you full-time, and you have the right to reside in the UK."]

child_evaluations.reasoning: Alex is 6 years old and therefore under 16. He lives with you full-time, making you responsible for him. You live in the UK and have the right to reside there. According to the eligibility criteria, you normally qualify for Child Benefit if you're responsible for a child under 16 and you live in the UK. All these conditions are satisfied, so Alex is eligible for Child Benefit.

response.overall_reasoning: You appear to be eligible for Child Benefit for Alex. You meet all the key requirements: you're responsible for a child under 16, the child lives with you full-time, and you have the right to reside in the UK."]
